attributes.content:
## Overview

The Blog Post card gives the workspace a structured home for blog entry with title, body, author, publish date, tags, featured image, and slug.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Blog Post usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Blog Post can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Note]]** -- Note can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Blog Post.
- **[[Article]]** -- Article can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Blog Post.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Blog Post into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Blog Post can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Blog Post can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Blog Post as the structured home for blog entry with title, body, author, publish date, tags, featured image, and slug and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Blog Post when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Blog Post stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Blog Post
cardInfo.summary: Blog entry with title, body, author, publish date, tags, featured image, and slug.
